comment_text.sn5.7:1.2: The younger sister of Cālā, her verses are at Thig 7.3.
comment_text.sn5.7:2.1: Māra mentions five of the sense-sphere heavens, omitting only the lowest of them, the realm of the Four Great Kings.
comment_text.sn5.7:3.1: These verses are attributed to Sīsūpacālā at Thig 8.1:5.1.

root_text.sn5.7:1.2: Atha kho upacālā bhikkhunī pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā …pe…
root_text.sn5.7:1.4: Atha kho māro pāpimā yena upacālā bhikkhunī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā upacālaṁ bhikkhuniṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn5.7:6.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ upacālā bhikkhunī”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn5.7:1.2: Then the nun Upacālā robed up in the morning …
translation_text.sn5.7:1.3: and sat at the root of a tree for the day’s meditation.
translation_text.sn5.7:1.4: Then Māra the Wicked went up to Upacālā and said to her,
translation_text.sn5.7:1.5: “Nun, where do you want to be reborn?”
translation_text.sn5.7:1.6: “I don’t want to be reborn anywhere, sir.”
translation_text.sn5.7:2.1: “There are the gods of the thirty-three, <j>and those of Yama;
translation_text.sn5.7:2.3: the gods who love to create,
translation_text.sn5.7:2.4: and the gods who control that imagined by others.
translation_text.sn5.7:2.5: Set your heart on such places,
translation_text.sn5.7:3.1: “The gods of the thirty-three, <j>and those of Yama;
translation_text.sn5.7:3.3: the gods who love to create,
translation_text.sn5.7:3.4: and the gods who control that imagined by others—
translation_text.sn5.7:3.5: they’re bound with the bonds of sensuality;
translation_text.sn5.7:3.6: they fall under your sway again.
translation_text.sn5.7:4.1: All the world is on fire,
translation_text.sn5.7:5.4: and not frequented by ordinary people.”
translation_text.sn5.7:6.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The nun Upacālā knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.

variant_text.sn5.7:4.1: Sabbo ādīpito → sabbo ādippito (bj); sabbova āditto (sya-all, km); sabbo ādipito (pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn5.7:5.1: apajjalitaṁ → ajalitaṁ (bj); acalitaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
